title: UA address decode roundtrip not tested in address generation
description: testnet_wallet_address_generation (e2e_testnet.rs lines 173-231) asserts the generated address starts with 'u1' but never decodes it back to verify the encoding is valid. sapling_address_as_testnet_ua calls ua.encode() but the result is only checked for prefix and non-emptiness. A malformed encoding could pass both checks. The test should call UnifiedAddress::decode (or zcash_address::ZcashAddress::decode) on the result and assert it round-trips to the same Sapling receiver bytes. This would catch bugs in the Receiver::Sapling construction (e.g. wrong byte length or wrong network).
close_reason: P3/P4 deferred: below stopping threshold (P0=0, P1=0, SEC7 says STOP)
